subject: fix(template-selector): defensively guard against splitSubSections stale-closure race
body:
Per pushback: don't track a fixable bug as TODO when I'm in the file.

The race trigger flagged that handleFileSelect captures splitSubSections
in its closure across the parseDocument await. In the current UI, the
toggle checkbox only renders inside ContractUploadPreview (preview
state), so no user-visible path exposes the staleness — the bug is
theoretical, not reachable. But the cost of guarding is 4 lines + a
useEffect, and if the toggle is ever surfaced in the upload tab itself
(e.g. a power-user pre-toggle), the call site stays correct without
having to be re-thought.

Mechanism: mirror splitSubSections into a ref, sync via useEffect, read
the ref's current value after the parseDocument await instead of the
closure's captured value.

No regression test: the race isn't reachable from current UI, so an
RTL test can't drive it without exposing internals. The mechanism is
visible in code review and the in-file comment is honest about its
defensive nature.

TODO.md: removed the "Minor UX race" item from Contract Upload
Enhancements — closed by this commit.

Tags: defensive, fix, race
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: custom-code-reduction
Semver: patch

title: defensively guard against splitSubSections stale-closure race
